query: We're about to open-weights release a fine-tuned BERT for medical-document classification. The model was fine-tuned on internal de-identified clinical notes from our hospital network. We plan to publish the weights and tokenizer publicly on Hugging Face. What goes in the model card?
expected_behavior: Flags the training-data leakage concern explicitly — clinical-note fine-tuning data, even if de-identified, raises memorization / training-data-extraction / membership-inference risk because the weights themselves are being released publicly, NOT just the predictions

Requires the out-of-scope section to explicitly enumerate clinical-decision use cases (diagnosis, prescribing, triage without a clinician in the loop) as out-of-scope, because downstream fine-tuners and integrators are unknown once weights are public

Requires the AIBOM / supply-chain section (dependencies, versions, hashes — at minimum the framework + pretrained backbone with revision hash + license + the fine-tuned model artifact hash) as MANDATORY for a public open-weights release, not optional
